name: Potential Flow of Execution Hijack
notes: This rule triggers when a dll is loaded or deleted from a suspicious folder or file.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regsvr32 Command Line Without DLL
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Spooler Service Binary Load
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Spooler Service File Deletion
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regsvr32 Command Line Without DLL, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Spooler Service File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Spooler Service Binary Load, BB:BehaviorDefinition: DLL Execution Via Register-cimprovider.exe
TD_list: APPLY Potential Flow of Execution Hijack on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regsvr32 Command Line Without DLL, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Spooler Service File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Spooler Service Binary Load, BB:BehaviorDefinition: DLL Execution Via Register-cimprovider.exe
RED_list: Event name: Potential Flow of Execution Hijack
Event description: Detects when a dll is loaded or deleted from a suspicious folder or file.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
